fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kentucky Licensed Dietitian (LD) Application — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for a Kentucky Licensed Dietitian (LD) credential. $50 application fee. Master's degree, CDR exam, and 1,000 hours of supervised practice required.
seo.keywords: Kentucky dietitian license, KY licensed dietitian requirements, Kentucky Board of Licensure Dietitians, Kentucky dietitian application fee

board.name: Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Kentucky. Dual licensure as a Dietitian and Nutritionist is also an option for the same $50 application fee.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of supervised practice.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

steps.title: Submit the Application via eServices
steps.description: Create an account on the Kentucky DPL eServices portal, submit the online application, and pay the $50 application fee.

steps.title: Provide Documentation
steps.description: Upload or mail a copy of your current active CDR registration card, official transcripts, and proof of supervised practice.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Must show your conferred master's degree. Can be sent securely via Clearinghouse to the board.
Documentation showing completion of at least 1,000 hours.

provisional_text: Kentucky does not offer a temporary or provisional license. Applicants must have passed the CDR examination prior to applying for licensure.
reciprocity_text: Kentucky allows licensure by endorsement. Applicants actively licensed in another state must provide verification of licensure and proof that they meet the same educational, supervised practice, and examination requirements (active CDR registration typically satisfies this).

faqs.question: How much does a Kentucky dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The application fee for initial licensure as a dietitian (or dual licensure as a dietitian/nutritionist) is $50.

faqs.question: When does the Kentucky license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Kentucky dietitian licenses expire annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $50. There is a 60-day grace period (until Dec 31st) with late fees applied.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements?
faqs.answer: Licensees must complete at least 15 hours of approved continuing education every year prior to the October 31st renewal deadline.
